An Evening With Ulysses S. Grant No Images? [Click here](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-e-jthhbyk-kujkulhluu-g/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jthhbyk-kujkulhluu-r/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jthhbyk-kujkulhluu-y/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jthhbyk-kujkulhluu-j/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jthhbyk-kujkulhluu-t/)
### [An Evening With Ulysses S. Grant](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jthhbyk-kujkulhluu-i/)
I considered writing this week's blog about President Trump's declaration of a national emergency, or which Democrat jumped into the presidential race most recently, or AOC's latest crackpot proposal, but decided instead to write about Ulysses S. Grant. Yes, Ulysses S. Grant.

Click the link below to read more. [Read this entry >>](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jthhbyk-kujkulhluu-d/) _Previous Weekly Posts_ [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jthhbyk-kujkulhluu-h/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jthhbyk-kujkulhluu-k/)
### [So Just How Radical Is This New Democratic Party?](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jthhbyk-kujkulhluu-u/)
Pretty darn radical. John F. Kennedy sure wouldn't recognize it. He believed instinctively in a strong America. In lower taxes to spur economic growth. In a robust foreign policy, and an adequate military to support it. In less government, not more. Remember "ask not what your country can do for you, ask what you can do for your country?" [Read this entry >>](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jthhbyk-kujkulhluu-o/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jthhbyk-kujkulhluu-b/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jthhbyk-kujkulhluu-n/)
### [Are We Headed for Another Shutdown?](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jthhbyk-kujkulhluu-p/)
Well the longest government shutdown in U.S. history is finally over. Of course we could be right back in the middle of another one in less than three weeks. So what's going to happen? [Read this entry >>](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jthhbyk-kujkulhluu-x/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jthhbyk-kujkulhluu-m/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jthhbyk-kujkulhluu-c/)
### [What the heck is a "Green New Deal"?](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jthhbyk-kujkulhluu-q/)
Well, the partial government shutdown drags on. Today is day 33 - the longest shutdown in U.S. history. We've seen Speaker Pelosi uninvite President Trump to the State of the Union. We've seen President Trump counterpunch, and cancel Pelosi's plane ride to the Middle East. subject: Will you join me and sign on?

body:
The kind of change I'm fighting for.
|
---

| | Nearly a third of U.S. households are overburdened by housing costs. Forty percent of adults can't cover an emergency expense of $400 without borrowing or selling something. Seven million Americans are three or more months behind on car payments. Over the last four decades, GDP, corporate profits, and executive salaries have all gone up -- thanks to the productivity of American workers. **But workers haven't shared in the economic growth they create.** For too many Americans hard work isn't paying off like it should. And I want to change that. **Together, we can:**
* Raise the minimum wage.
* Ensure workers are paid for overtime.
* Pass equal pay laws.
* Make it easier for workers to join a union, so they can collectively bargain for better wages, benefits, and conditions.

[**Peter, I'm asking for your support. Sign on to support my Dignity of Work plan for progressive change >>**](https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/page/m/302ffc28/4462dc26/8e23d170/201ed447/238828738/VEsH/ "https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/e190220z") All work has dignity, and all work should be rewarded. Together, we can make that happen. subject: STRIKE!
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY MIKE SIEGEL FOR CONGRESS](https://click
body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/4956892/41607079/683764388?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82MzkwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkZjg0ODdiNC1mZTM1LWU5MTEtYjhiMy0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=qKMbJdJ_S2iTy0hcp8bNLHdGA5vqAO7M_LGl4OfdONM=)
---
Peter, Today the teachers in Oakland, California go on strike. My heart is with them. 20 years ago, my wife and I started our professional careers as teachers in Oakland. I taught third grade in an overcrowded elementary school, working in a dilapidated “portable” classroom that was 40 years old. We were optimistic and overwhelmed and underpaid, doing whatever we could to support students who in many ways were set up for failure. Unfortunately, our friends who started with us, who are now in their 20th year in the Oakland schools, are facing many of the same challenges today: large class sizes, rock-bottom wages, no counselors or librarians, and local and state officials who are unable or unwilling to make improvements.

**[When I am in Congress, I will fight for stronger labor laws that will allow for workers to have a voice. Help send me to Congress to work for public school teachers.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/4956893/41607080/-917578712?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82MzkwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkZjg0ODdiNC1mZTM1LWU5MTEtYjhiMy0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=qKMbJdJ_S2iTy0hcp8bNLHdGA5vqAO7M_LGl4OfdONM=&refcode=em022119&emci=de8487b4-fe35-e911-b8b3-281878391efb&emdi=df8487b4-fe35-e911-b8b3-281878391efb&ceid=6233172)** | [Donate Now](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/4956894/41607081/-917578712?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82MzkwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkZjg0ODdiNC1mZTM1LWU5MTEtYjhiMy0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=qKMbJdJ_S2iTy0hcp8bNLHdGA5vqAO7M_LGl4OfdONM=&refcode=em022119&emci=de8487b4-fe35-e911-b8b3-281878391efb&emdi=df8487b4-fe35-e911-b8b3-281878391efb&ceid=6233172)
---
In Texas, in California, in Colorado and West Virginia, our public schools are in crisis. Our governments refuse to provide sufficient funding, and districts serving low income students are trapped in a vicious cycle. Poor conditions lead to low student performance, which leads to punitive sanctions and school closures, which drives students to leave for charter schools that can operate without accountability or oversight. And when these students leave, so does the per-pupil funding, which exacerbates the crisis.
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/4956895/41607082/203122784?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82MzkwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkZjg0ODdiNC1mZTM1LWU5MTEtYjhiMy0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=qKMbJdJ_S2iTy0hcp8bNLHdGA5vqAO7M_LGl4OfdONM=&refcode=em022119)
Unfortunately, the institutional failure of public education is by design. For a variety of reasons, the powers-that-be don’t value public school funding. Some folks don’t want to pay to educate “other people’s children.” Some folks would rather create a profit opportunity for charter and private schools. Some folks want to weaken teachers unions. And some folks understand that an educated populace will be better equipped to advocate for social change, which would undermine those who benefit from the status quo.
Whatever the reasons, we have to understand that the attack on public education is an attack on democracy. Public schools embody the American Dream of equal opportunity. That a student can go to school, work hard, have success, and become whatever she or he wants to be. But when public education fails, the promise of equal opportunity fails, and the promise of this country fails.

**[Our representatives in Congress don't value the mentally, physically, and emotionally taxing work teachers deal with every day — I do. Join our campaign so I can fight for teachers in DC!](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/4956896/41607083/-917578712?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82MzkwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkZjg0ODdiNC1mZTM1LWU5MTEtYjhiMy0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=qKMbJdJ_S2iTy0hcp8bNLHdGA5vqAO7M_LGl4OfdONM=&refcode=em022119&emci=de8487b4-fe35-e911-b8b3-281878391efb&emdi=df8487b4-fe35-e911-b8b3-281878391efb&ceid=6233172)**
So we rise today with Oakland’s teachers, who by fighting for better working conditions are rising up for their students and, by extension, for all of us.
Educating our nation’s children is one of the most important jobs we have.
Let’s honor teachers and support them as they continue the essential work of building our democratic future.
Mike

name: David Duhalde, Our Revolution
subject: Bernie's running, here's what's next
body:
Senator Bernie Sanders is running for President in 2020 and our grassroots movement is ready for action.
# [](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7a599/bed43eb/9c993f43/75fb024a/4034205491/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/ourrevolution?refcode=email-header-OR")
Senator Bernie Sanders is running for president in 2020 and our grassroots movement is ready for action. Your support has built a nationwide progressive infrastructure with over 600 local groups that are ready to hit the ground running.
[**Chip in $5 so we can begin deploying materials to volunteers across the country.**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7a599/bed43eb/9c993f43/75fb0275/4034205491/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/or-2020?refcode=em190221-full&noskip=true")
****[DONATE»](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7a599/bed43eb/9c993f43/75fb0275/4034205491/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/or-2020?refcode=em190221-full&noskip=true")****
Friends,
Your support has given this movement the most important head start we could hope for: an organized grassroots base spanning the country and ready for action.
Here's what you're helping us build:
* Direct outreach programs for phone banking, texting, and door knocking.
* One-on-one trainings with group leaders and volunteers.
* Event hosting guides for anyone and everyone interested in hosting an event.

[**A $5 contribution right now will support our work and ensure the deployment of these resources.**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7a599/bed43eb/9c993f43/75fb0275/4034205491/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/or-2020?refcode=em190221-full&noskip=true")
**The political and financial establishment want anyone but Bernie Sanders to be the Democratic nominee in 2020.** They need you to believe that he cannot defeat Trump -- but the truth is that a majority of Americans support Bernie's policies.
Medicare for All, a Green New Deal, College for All, a $15 minimum wage and so many other progressive issues are the reasons millions of you across the country brought your voices together to fight for a candidate who will not waver in putting the people and the planet before profits.

subject: Border Apprehensions Jump 84 Percent, 606,000 Expected This Year
body:
"January saw a surprising surge of 22,000 more apprehensions of illegal immigrants at southwest border crossings over January 2018." | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=ac185f0fd6&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **Border Apprehensions Jump 84 Percent, 606,000 Expected This Year**

“January saw a surprising surge of 22,000 more apprehensions of illegal immigrants at southwest border crossings over January 2018, prompting a key predictor to suggest that border officers will make over 600,000 apprehensions this year,” Paul Bedard reports for the _Washington Examiner_. “The year-over-year increase was 84 percent, from 25,975 in January 2018 to 47,893 last January, according to U.S. Customs and Border Protection.”

Princeton Policy Advisors’ Steven Kopits believes the surge suggests that more illegal immigrants could be heading to the border than expected. “Buckle your seat belts: 2019 could be a wild ride at the southwest border,” he says.

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=e88625f5d4&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| “The number of people who applied for jobless benefits in mid-February fell sharply and returned near post-recession lows, suggesting the U.S. labor market is still going strong and keeping the economy on firm footing,” Jeffry Bartash writes in _[MarketWatch](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=a6a8d0f9cc&e=3ee4985eea)_. The big picture: “The U.S. is still adding new jobs at a rapid clip to keep the unemployment rate near the lowest level in decades.”
---
|
---
| “President Trump [tweeted](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=31975960dd&e=3ee4985eea) Wednesday that a new section of the border wall just erected in New Mexico, and he was telling the truth,” Eddie Scarry reports in the _[Washington Examiner](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=76e2141e1a&e=3ee4985eea)_. U.S. Customs and Border Protection confirmed that the wall referenced was a “project announced in April 2018 to build 20 miles of new steel wall that replaced small fencing that prevents vehicle crossing. The new barriers are from 18 to 30 feet high, depending on the terrain.”
---
|
---
| “After two years of the Trump presidency, America’s economic engine has been reignited. Since President Trump took office nearly five million jobs have been created, including more than 450,000 jobs in the manufacturing sector,” Rep. Tim Walberg (R-MI) writes in _[The Detroit News](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8debaeab18&e=3ee4985eea)_. “In just a couple years, the economy has made significant strides. However, the rush by some leaders on the left to abandon free enterprise principles and embrace the tenets of socialism threatens this very progress.”
---
|

[Privacy Policy](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=647c15b80a&e=3ee4985eea) | [Contact the White House](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=3c8ba11dd0&e=3ee4985eea) | [Unsubscribe](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8cafb01033&e=3ee4985eea&c=475cae6c85)
---
| | | The White House * 1600 Pennsylvania Ave NW * Washington, DC 20500 * USA · 202-456-1111
---

name: Sherrod Brown
subject: Trump lied.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Sherrod Brown
body:
There's what Trump says about auto jobs, and then there's reality.
|
---

| | | | **PRESIDENT TRUMP** : "We're most proud of the fact - you look at the car companies, they're moving back, they're going into Michigan, they're going into Pennsylvania, they're going back to Ohio, so many companies are coming back." \-- Campaign Rally, 2/2019
---
| **REALITY** : "Lordstown is losing auto factory jobs as General Motors is set to shutter a large plant in town in a month." \-- _Washington Post_ , 2/6/2019
---
| "The sting from a major restructuring at General Motors and its planned closings of five North American factories in the coming months is **putting thousands of jobs at auto parts suppliers at stake."** \-- _Digital Journal_ , 2/2019
---
President Trump's traveling around the country, giving speech after speech, **lying to American workers.**
Here's the truth: Trump handed companies like GM massive tax breaks in the law he and the Republicans championed last year. He promised companies would use those windfalls to expand in the U.S., giving more people the jobs they need. Instead, GM is laying off American workers and expanding their facilities in Mexico.
**Workers in Ohio and across the country deserve better.** They deserve a president who doesn't lie to their faces while handing their tax dollars to huge corporations. And they deserve economic policies that reward their labor and loyalty -- and don't leave them facing layoffs.
[**Our Dignity of Work agenda is all about how we create a system that gives hardworking people a fair shot to get ahead. Check it out, and then show your support by signing on. >>**](https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/page/m/302ffc2f/4462dc26/8e3e8505/201ed518/769571889/VEsH/ "https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/e190221a")
With gratitude,
Sherrod

subject: What Beto did over the weekend
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY BETO FOR TEXAS
body:
Beto hit the road over the weekend to visit communities across the midwest
and once again hear what’s on the minds of people throughout the country.
The highlight of the trip was having the opportunity to meet with hundreds
of students at Milwaukee Area Technical College and the University of
Wisconsin in Madison.

Beto also had the chance to speak with various news outlets along the way,
and we wanted to share five of our favorite articles with you:

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/16825?t=1001&akid=24938%2E444211%2EpLT7yP ]Milwaukee Journal Sentinel: Beto O’Rourke Visits Students at MATC
[Photos]

Former U.S. Rep. Beto O’Rourke of Texas greeted students at Milwaukee Area
Technical College during a visit to Wisconsin. He listened to MATC
students talk about their concerns for getting jobs after graduation and
spoke about inequitable wealth distribution, free tuition, gun safety and
the environment. [ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/16825?t=1002&akid=24938%2E444211%2EpLT7yP ]Read here >>

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/16826?t=1003&akid=24938%2E444211%2EpLT7yP ]The Badger Herald: At UW Visit Beto O’Rourke Discusses Youth
Engagement, Immigration, 2020

O’Rourke went on to say that he wants to listen to everyone, particularly
student voices, because that is what will help him determine how to best
help the country. The event was so well attended that overflow rooms were
provided for students unable to get tickets. O’Rourke greeted and took a
photo with every student in attendance, including those in the overflow
rooms. [ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/16826?t=1004&akid=24938%2E444211%2EpLT7yP ]Read here >>

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/16827?t=1005&akid=24938%2E444211%2EpLT7yP ]Fox-6 News: ‘Take the Time to Learn:’ Beto O’Rourke Visits Milwaukee,
Madison While Debating Presidential Run

The stop was part of his cross-country listening tour, meeting with
students and different communities in an effort to help decide whether or
not to run for president. "I want to find out how they're going to make
America a better place," said O'Rourke. "And I want to take my
inspiration, direction and guidance from them." From reducing gun violence
to fighting the opioid epidemic, students at MATC met with O'Rourke to
offer their thoughts on a number of issues concerning them. [ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/16827?t=1006&akid=24938%2E444211%2EpLT7yP ]Read here
>>

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/16828?t=1007&akid=24938%2E444211%2EpLT7yP ]Wisconsin Public Radio: In National Tour of Colleges, Beto O’Rourke
Visits MATC in Milwaukee

The former congressman from Texas spent more than an hour with MATC
students in downtown Milwaukee talking about topics ranging from gun
control to opioid abuse before meeting with members of the media. O’Rourke
said he wanted to learn more about the role of technical colleges in
communities. He said he also wanted to gain a better perspective on what
it's like to live in, work in, learn in, grow up in and raise a family in
the Milwaukee area. He said being at MATC was "very powerful." [ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/16828?t=1008&akid=24938%2E444211%2EpLT7yP ]Read
here >>

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/16829?t=1009&akid=24938%2E444211%2EpLT7yP ]Telemundo Wisconsin: Beto O’Rourke en gira por medio oeste mientras
piensa posible candidatura presidencial

"Quiero entender las historias de gente que viven aquí en Milwaukee, otras
partes del país para conocer las quejas, oportunidades, ideas para el
futuro de los estados unidos." Beto dice que es un momento en la política
emocionante. Añadiendo que aunque tendremos algunos retos a lo largo del
camino, que valen la pena para asegurar un mejor futuro para todos.
[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/16829?t=1010&akid=24938%2E444211%2EpLT7yP ]Leer aqui >>

Thanks for keeping up with Beto’s road trip and sharing these great
headlines.

Talk soon,

Team Beto

Contribute to Beto's campaign: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/beto-email?refcode=em20190221footer&t=1011&refcode2=24938_444211_pLT7yP&akid=24938%2E444211%2EpLT7yP

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to act. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://act.betofortexas.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1012&akid=24938%2E444211%2EpLT7yP

P.O. Box 3628 El Paso, TX 79923
